title: /kernel
summary_text: **Concrete Requirements** - First acknowledgement must be emitted only after lanes are active, and it must include exactly this header structure: `Kernel orchestration is active for this session.` `Bootstrap target: 6+ lanes (minimum 6).` `Active models: ...` (live-evidence models only) `Lane manifest:` - Manifest must include at least 6 active lanes (not planned/pending/failed), each with provider + role classification (`codex`/`glm`/`specialist`), active agent label, and concrete subagent label (`subagentN` or `none`). - Before that first acknowledgement, bootstrap receipt must be written with: `bash scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write <lane_count> <providers_csv> <normal|degraded-allowed>` - At receipt write time, these must be set: `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV` `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_MANIFEST_LANE_COUNT` `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_AGENT_LABELS=true` `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_SUBAGENT_LABELS=true` - Healthy shape must be either: `normal = codex + glm + specialist` or `degraded-allowed = codex + specialist + specialist` (only after `glm` fails twice in the same run). - If neither valid shape is possible, or if 6 active lanes cannot be sustained, fail closed (`BLOCKED`), not “healthy”. **Concrete Risks** - Acknowledging Kernel active before writing receipt/evidence is a contract violation. - Counting planned/unlabeled/failed lanes toward the 6-lane minimum invalidates the manifest. - Listing models in `Active models:` without live run evidence creates false compliance. - Entering `degraded-allowed` without two same-run `glm` failures is non-compliant. - Returning from degraded to normal within the same run is non-compliant (must wait for next `KERNEL_RUN_ID`). - In normal mode, missing `glm`, missing specialist, missing `glm` credentials, or lane count `<6` must hard-fail; continuing anyway is a fail-closed breach.
